description: Definitions for the 10 named Pre-Flight Checks (Requirement 6.1). Loaded by discover-preflight.md. Every check is computed unconditionally during Discover, before Recommend has run (Requirement 6.2) - applies_to and adapter_generation are consulted at report-render time to filter/reframe, never to gate computation.

checks.title: Cached-route x middleware intersection
checks.detection: discovery.json's middleware_analysis.per_matcher_pattern[] (the authoritative, per-pattern classification - NOT the collapsed middleware_analysis.classification) intersected, PER PATTERN, with static/ISR/CDN-cacheable routes; a non-empty intersection on ANY pattern triggers this check
checks.severity_rule: For each matcher pattern that intersects a cacheable route, read THAT pattern's own classification (never the whole file's collapsed classification, since different patterns in one middleware.ts commonly do different things). HIGH when the intersecting pattern's classification is auth gating, A/B bucketing, geo-redirects, or a per-request rewrite (users could receive cached pages that skipped an auth check; A/B collapses to one variant). LOW when the intersecting pattern's classification is header decoration or logging only. When multiple patterns intersect cacheable routes with different classifications, severity is the MAX across all intersecting patterns, and the finding's detail must name the specific pattern/route intersection that drove the HIGH verdict - never imply the whole file is HIGH-classified when only one pattern is.
checks.rationale: On Vercel, middleware runs at the edge before the CDN and executes even on cached requests. On OpenNext, middleware is bundled into the server function; on Fargate, it runs inside next start. Either way, any CloudFront-cached response skips middleware entirely - this applies to every AWS outcome, not just OpenNext. Generation-independent: reflects CDN-in-front-of-origin architecture, not build-output reverse-engineering (Requirement 6.5).
remediations: OpenNext external middleware option (Outcome A only)
CloudFront Functions for simple header/redirect logic (Outcome B)

checks.title: Geo/IP header dependence
checks.detection: grep middleware and server code for Vercel-injected geo headers (x-vercel-ip-country, x-vercel-ip-city, x-vercel-ip-latitude, etc.)
checks.rationale: Vercel-injected geo/IP headers have no AWS equivalent by default; CloudFront provides analogous headers under different names and requires an explicit header-mapping configuration.
remediations: Emit a CloudFront-headers mapping in the scaffold (scaffold-peripherals.md)
Rewrite server code to read the CloudFront-equivalent header names

checks.title: Monorepo lockfile conflicts
checks.detection: more than one lockfile found at repo root (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, bun.lockb) - see prescan-scan.md lockfile_census
checks.severity_rule: HIGH (build-breaking) - multiple lockfiles cause missing-module errors in Lambda
checks.rationale: Container builds (Outcome B) are tolerant of multiple lockfiles; this finding is suppressed on B.
remediations: Remove all but one lockfile and re-install
Configure the monorepo's package manager to produce a single canonical lockfile

checks.title: Yarn packageManager pin
checks.detection: package.json packageManager field starts with 'yarn' - see prescan-scan.md package_manager
checks.rationale: packageManager: yarn correlates with OpenNext build failures.
remediations: Pin to a known-working Yarn version
Switch to pnpm or npm for the OpenNext build path

checks.title: sharp as a direct dependency
checks.detection: sharp present in package.json dependencies or devDependencies - see prescan-scan.md has_sharp_dependency
checks.rationale: OpenNext's image optimization ships its own sharp; a duplicate direct dependency causes bloat/conflicts. On Outcome B, sharp is simply a container dependency - no finding.
remediations: Remove the direct sharp dependency and let OpenNext's bundled version handle image optimization

checks.title: Bundle contamination
checks.detection: known-problematic packages incorrectly traced into the server bundle (heuristic package-name list, checked against next.config.js output tracing)
checks.rationale: Server-only or dev-only packages accidentally bundled into Lambda functions bloat cold-start time and package size.
remediations: Add the offending packages to next.config.js outputFileTracingExcludes

checks.title: Streaming routes with potentially-empty bodies
checks.detection: route handlers using streaming responses (ReadableStream, Response with a stream body) where the stream can legitimately emit zero bytes
checks.rationale: Lambda streaming hangs on empty response bodies; an env-var workaround exists. next start behind an ALB (Outcome B) streams natively and is not affected.
remediations: Apply the documented Lambda streaming env-var workaround
Ensure streaming handlers always emit at least one byte before completion

checks.title: ISR / on-demand revalidation completeness
checks.detection: revalidateTag / revalidatePath / route-segment revalidate usage detected in source configs

severity_rule_by_outcome.A: HIGH if the scaffold omits either the tag cache or the revalidation queue - they must be provisioned together, not just the incremental cache.
severity_rule_by_outcome.B: Reframed entirely: a single-container next start handles ISR in-process, but MULTI-INSTANCE Fargate needs a shared cache handler (or dropping ISR for cache-control headers + CloudFront invalidation). HIGH only if autoscaling >1 task with ISR present and no shared cache strategy.

remediations: Outcome A: provision the tag cache AND revalidation queue together (scaffold-opennext.md)
Outcome B: add a shared cache handler for multi-instance deployments, or drop ISR in favor of cache-control headers + CloudFront invalidation

checks.title: Build environment consistency
checks.detection: no direct detection - advisory check, always surfaced when Outcome A applies
checks.rationale: Local vs. CI OpenNext builds can chunk-mismatch, causing site-wide 500s. On Outcome B, generic container-build hygiene applies - advisory only, same severity.
remediations: Deploy only from CI, never from a local OpenNext build

checks.title: Uncached high-invocation routes (cost driver flag)
checks.detection: cross-reference route cache config (from discover-configs.md) against usage API invocation counts (from discover-api.md, or log-drain data from discover-probe.md/Tier 2 if available)
checks.rationale: An uncached API route firing multiple times per page view is both a migration driver and a quantifiable savings line - the one place thin Vercel billing data still yields a concrete number. Any dollar figure this check produces must still be labeled 'estimated monthly' per Requirement 9.6, even though full cost estimation is deferred to v2.
remediations: Add appropriate caching (revalidate, Cache-Control headers) to the flagged route(s)
